specific_location: Crow Valley Campground, near the house wren tree (near the SW corner), or near the watering hole.
description: The bird was the size of a western tanager, but lacked wingbars. Beak seemed larger than a western tanager by a little bit, but not to the extent that a Summer Tanager would be. The beak color was a yellowy-pale color. Complete eye-ring was evident. Tail was yellow as well as undertail. Back was a dull olivey-yellow that did not contrast much with yellow of the chest and head.Behaviors: Only saw the bird long enough to snap two photos of it. It was high up in a tree, was not vocalizing, and flew off moments after the second photo was taken.Call: SilentPlumage: Adult
